maml:description.maml:para: Get a cacheable report.
Retrieve data for a specific version of a type of report. While functionally identical to New-LegacyReport, this alternative GET operation specifies all request data as query parameters.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An alternative to `-ObjectIDs`, enabling this generates a report that includes all IDs available for the specified object type (each report supports only one specific object type). This parameter is ignored if the request also specifies a set of `-ObjectIDs`.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.

The criteria to filter the report's data. The set of available filters depends on the type of report. Separate each filter name and value with an equals (`=`) character, and separate various name-value pairs with commas (`,`). To specify more than one filter value, repeat the filter name.
The duration of each data record, either `FIVE_MINUTES`, `HOUR`, `DAY`, `WEEK`, or `MONTH`. Support for specific interval values may vary by report type.
A comma-separated list of metrics to include in the report, otherwise all metrics are retrieved if omitted. The set of available metrics depends on the type of report.
The type of report.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
The version of the report type.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.

The criteria to filter the report's data. The set of available filters depends on the type of report. Separate each filter name and value with an equals (`=`) character, and separate various name-value pairs with commas (`,`). To specify more than one filter value, repeat the filter name.
The duration of each data record, either `FIVE_MINUTES`, `HOUR`, `DAY`, `WEEK`, or `MONTH`. Support for specific interval values may vary by report type.
A comma-separated list of metrics to include in the report, otherwise all metrics are retrieved if omitted. The set of available metrics depends on the type of report.
The type of report.
An alternative to `-AllObjectIDs`, specifies the set of unique IDs for the given object type you want to report on, formatted as a comma-delimited list.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
The version of the report type.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An alternative to `-ObjectIDs`, enabling this generates a report that includes all IDs available for the specified object type (each report supports only one specific object type). This parameter is ignored if the request also specifies a set of `-ObjectIDs`.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.

The criteria to filter the report's data. The set of available filters depends on the type of report. Separate each filter name and value with an equals (`=`) character, and separate various name-value pairs with commas (`,`). To specify more than one filter value, repeat the filter name.
The duration of each data record, either `FIVE_MINUTES`, `HOUR`, `DAY`, `WEEK`, or `MONTH`. Support for specific interval values may vary by report type.
A comma-separated list of metrics to include in the report, otherwise all metrics are retrieved if omitted. The set of available metrics depends on the type of report.
The type of report.
An alternative to `-AllObjectIDs`, specifies the set of unique IDs for the given object type you want to report on, formatted as a comma-delimited list.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
The version of the report type.
Get a report type.
Get a specific version of a report type.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The type of report.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The version of the report type.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
When enabled, retrieves deprecated reports.
When enabled, retrieves unavailable reports.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The type of report.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
When enabled, retrieves deprecated reports.
When enabled, retrieves unavailable reports.
The version of the report type.
List versions.
List all versions for a specific report type.
The type of report.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
When enabled, retrieves deprecated reports.
When enabled, retrieves unavailable reports.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The type of report.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
When enabled, retrieves deprecated reports.
When enabled, retrieves unavailable reports.
Get report options.
Returns available options to generate a single report, or retrieve available reports by Product Family and Reporting Area.
An individual report name.
The area of products.
The grouping of reports related to the same area.
Unique identifier of the previous asynchronous request to fetch report data.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The area of products.
Unique identifier of the previous asynchronous request to fetch report data.
An individual report name.
The grouping of reports related to the same area.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
List reporting areas.
List all reporting areas, which you can use to filter your request with Get-ReportType.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
List reporting product families.
List all product families, which you can use to filter your request with Get-ReportingType.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Get or list report types.
Gets all report types or retrieves a specific version of a report type.
The area of products.
The grouping of reports related to the same area.
An individual report name.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The area of products.
An individual report name.
The grouping of reports related to the same area.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
Generate a report.

Produce a report in either JSON or CSV format for a specific version of a type of report. In CSV data, values with commas or space characters are quoted. Query parameters specify the range of data and the aggregation interval for each record.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Label used as title for grouped record.
For reports that allow non-top values to cluster, this specifies the number of top values to report on separately.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.
The duration of each data record, either `FIVE_MINUTES`, `HOUR`, `DAY`, `WEEK`, or `MONTH`. Support for specific interval values may vary by report type.
The type of report.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
The version of the report type.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Label used as title for grouped record.
For reports that allow non-top values to cluster, this specifies the number of top values to report on separately.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.

The criteria to filter the report's data. The set of available filters depends on the type of report. Separate each filter name and value with an equals (`=`) character, and separate various name-value pairs with commas (`,`). To specify more than one filter value, repeat the filter name.
The duration of each data record, either `FIVE_MINUTES`, `HOUR`, `DAY`, `WEEK`, or `MONTH`. Support for specific interval values may vary by report type.
A comma-separated list of metrics to include in the report, otherwise all metrics are retrieved if omitted. The set of available metrics depends on the type of report.
The type of report.
An alternative to `-AllObjectIDs`, specifies the set of unique IDs for the given object type you want to report on, formatted as a comma-delimited list.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
The version of the report type.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Label used as title for grouped record.
For reports that allow non-top values to cluster, this specifies the number of top values to report on separately.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.

The criteria to filter the report's data. The set of available filters depends on the type of report. Separate each filter name and value with an equals (`=`) character, and separate various name-value pairs with commas (`,`). To specify more than one filter value, repeat the filter name.
The duration of each data record, either `FIVE_MINUTES`, `HOUR`, `DAY`, `WEEK`, or `MONTH`. Support for specific interval values may vary by report type.
A comma-separated list of metrics to include in the report, otherwise all metrics are retrieved if omitted. The set of available metrics depends on the type of report.
The type of report.
An alternative to `-AllObjectIDs`, specifies the set of unique IDs for the given object type you want to report on, formatted as a comma-delimited list.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
The version of the report type.
Generate a report.

Produces a report in either JSON or CSV format for a specific version of a report type. While functionally it's identical to the `Get-Report` operation, this operation specifies all request data as a request body, and therefore, it's better suited for very large datasets.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Enables the asynchronous flow to get the data. By default, `false`.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.

The size of a page in an async query. By default, the page size is the report's maximum number of returned data points, configured by the `dataPointsLimit` setting. If a report doesn't have this setting, the default value is `50000`.
The area of products.
An individual report name.
The grouping of reports related to the same area.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Enables the asynchronous flow to get the data. By default, `false`.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.

The size of a page in an async query. By default, the page size is the report's maximum number of returned data points, configured by the `dataPointsLimit` setting. If a report doesn't have this setting, the default value is `50000`.
The area of products.
An individual report name.
The grouping of reports related to the same area.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.

Predefined time range, either `LAST_15_MINUTES`, `LAST_30_MINUTES`, `LAST_1_HOUR`, `LAST_3_HOURS`, `LAST_6_HOURS`, `LAST_12_HOURS`, `LAST_1_DAY`, `LAST_2_DAYS`, `LAST_1_WEEK`, `LAST_30_DAYS`, or `LAST_90_DAYS`. Use this as an alternative to a more flexible `start` and `end` time range. Support for specific time range values may vary by report type. See the report documentation under Available reports (ref:available-reports).

An account credential key that lets you move between accounts when using an API client enabled for multiple accounts. To find account switch keys, use [Get-AccountSwitchKey](https://techdocs.akamai.com/powershell/docs/get-accountswitchkey).
Enables the asynchronous flow to get the data. By default, `false`.
A request body, either as a `PSCustomObject` or a string. This parameter supports pipelining.
Your EdgeGrid resource file to authenticate your command. Defaults to `~/.edgerc`.
The end of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with a timezone. Any data that matches the `end` value's timestamp is excluded from the report.

The size of a page in an async query. By default, the page size is the report's maximum number of returned data points, configured by the `dataPointsLimit` setting. If a report doesn't have this setting, the default value is `50000`.
The area of products.
An individual report name.
The grouping of reports related to the same area.
The section name in your EdgeGrid resource file to retrieve authentication credentials from. Defaults to `default`.
The start of the reported period as an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.

Predefined time range, either `LAST_15_MINUTES`, `LAST_30_MINUTES`, `LAST_1_HOUR`, `LAST_3_HOURS`, `LAST_6_HOURS`, `LAST_12_HOURS`, `LAST_1_DAY`, `LAST_2_DAYS`, `LAST_1_WEEK`, `LAST_30_DAYS`, or `LAST_90_DAYS`. Use this as an alternative to a more flexible `start` and `end` time range. Support for specific time range values may vary by report type. See the report documentation under Available reports (ref:available-reports).
